source_pr.title: ddl/ingest: unregister backend after job cancelled
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/44205

Problem Summary:

Previously, we unregister the add index job in the step of rolling back. However, for indexes in the "NONE" state, there is no rollback step.

### What is changed and how it works?

Unregister backend after job cancelled.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: DDL ingest backend context was not unregistered on job cancel in `StateNone`, leaving `LitBackCtxMgr` unavailable for subsequent operations/tests.
failure_signature: `ingest.LitBackCtxMgr` remains registered after cancel on `StateNone` (no rollback step), so `CheckAvailable()` can fail.
root_cause_explanation: When an ingest add-index DDL job is cancelled at `StateNone`, there may be no rollback step, so the backend context was not unregistered. That leaves the global ingest backend manager (`ingest.LitBackCtxMgr`) in a polluted state, which can break subsequent tests/DDL operations. This PR fixes it by ensuring `Unregister(job.ID)` is executed on all cancellation/rollback conversion paths (with nil-guarding) and adds an integration test that cancels a NONE-state job and asserts `LitBackCtxMgr.CheckAvailable()` returns true.
fix_pattern: Ensure global backend managers are unregistered/cleaned up on all cancel/rollback paths (including NONE-state cancel) and add a regression test asserting `CheckAvailable()`.

evidence: files: ddl/index.go, ddl/ingest/BUILD.bazel, ddl/ingest/integration_test.go, ddl/ingest/mock.go, ddl/rollingback.go
